nameMeanings.name: Jermyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   German   or   Jeremy  .

variants.name: German
meanings: English: ethnic name from Old French germain ‘German’ (from Latin Germanus). This sometimes denoted an actual immigrant from Germany, but was also used to refer to a person who had trade or other connections with German-speaking lands. The Latin word Germanus is of obscure and disputed origin; the most plausible of the etymologies that have been proposed is that the people were originally known as the ‘spear-men’, with ancient Germanic gēr, gār ‘spear’ as the first element.

Spanish and Hungarian (Germán); English (of Norman origin) and German: from the personal name Spanish and Hungarian Germán, Old French Germain, German German, from Latin Germanus (see Germain ), a Christian saint's name.
Americanized form of German   Germann   1, a name of ancient Germanic origin.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Simon , now rare, but more common in the Middle Ages, when it gave rise to the surnames Simms and Simpson.

variants.name: Simon
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Jermyn II was born in 1403, in Rushbrooke, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter with Lady Margaret Leyman. He died in 1480, in his hometown, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Jermyn II est né en 1403, à Rushbrooke, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Lady Margaret Leyman. Il est décédé en 1480, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 77 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Jermyn II wurde 1403 in Rushbrooke, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mit Lady Margaret Leyman mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1480 im Alter von 77 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Jermyn II è nato il nel 1403 nel Rushbrooke, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Lady Margaret Leyman. Morì nel 1480, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 77 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Jermyn IIは1403年にイギリスイギリススフォルクルシュブルークで生まれた。彼はLady Margaret Leymanとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1480年に故郷で77歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Jermyn II(은)는 1403년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 러스h브룩에서 태어났다. 그는 Lady Margaret Leyman(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1480년 그의 고향에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Jermyn II nasceu em 1403, em Rushbrooke, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Lady Margaret Leyman. Ele faleceu em 1480, em sua cidade natal, com 77 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Jermyn II родился в 1403 г. в местности рушбрук, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Lady Margaret Leyman. Он умер в 1480 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 77 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Jermyn II nació en 1403, en Rushbrooke, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Lady Margaret Leyman. Murió en 1480, en su pueblo, a la edad de 77 años.
